name: caen-libs

metadata.name: caen-libs
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: Official Python binding for CAEN VMELib, CAEN Comm, CAEN PLU and CAEN HV Wrapper libraries.

documentation:
# caen-libs
Official Python binding for CAEN VMELib, CAEN Comm, CAEN PLU and CAEN HV Wrapper libraries.

## Install
You need to install the latest version of the libraries from [the CAEN website](https://www.caen.it/subfamilies/software-libraries/).

Then, install this module and have fun:

    pip install caen-libs

## Examples
Few examples may be found on the [`examples`](https://github.com/caenspa/py-caen-libs/tree/main/examples) folder of the project repository.

## Copyright notice
Copyright © 2024 CAEN SpA

The caen-libs module is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
modify it under the terms of the **GNU Lesser General Public
License** as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

The caen-libs module is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU
Lesser General Public License for more details.

You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public License along
with the caen-libs module; if not, see https://www.gnu.org/licenses/.

The license applies to the caen-libs module source as a whole, though
individual source files can have a different license which is required to be
compatible with the GNU Lesser General Public Library, version 3.

## References
Requirements and documentation can be found at https://www.caen.it/subfamilies/software-libraries/.

## Support
For technical support, go to https://www.caen.it/mycaen/support/ (login and
MyCAEN+ account required).

functions.name: caen_libs._cache.cached
documentation:
LRU cache decorator that keeps a weak reference to self.

To be used as decorator on methods that are known to return always
the same value. This can improve the performances of some methods by
a factor > 1000.
This wrapper using weak references is required: functools.lru_cache
holds a reference to all arguments: using directly on the methods it
would hold a reference to self, introducing subdle memory leaks.

@sa https://stackoverflow.com/a/68052994/3287591
Typing support for decorators comes with Python 3.10.

functions.name: caen_libs._cache.clear
documentation:
LRU cache decorator that clear cache.

To be used as decorator on methods that are known to invalidate the
cache.

classes.name: caen_libs._cache.Manager

methods.name: caen_libs._cache.Manager.clear_all
documentation: Invoke `cache_clear` on all functions in the list

A simple list of functions returned by `@lru_cache` decorator.

To be used with the optional parameter @p cache_manager of
lru_cache_method(), that will store a reference to the cached
function inside this list. This is a typing-safe way to call
`cache_clear` and `cache_info` of the internal cached functions,
even if not exposed directly by the inner function returned by
lru_cache_method().

functions.name: caen_libs._string.from_char
documentation:
Split a buffer into a list of N string.
Strings are separated by the null terminator:
    e.g. S1� STR2� (n_str == 2)
String sizes are not bounded.
For ct.c_char and arrays of it.

functions.name: caen_libs._string.from_char_p
documentation:
Same of from_char.
For pointers to ct.c_char, to avoid dereferences in case of zero size.

functions.name: caen_libs._string.from_char_array
documentation:
Split a buffer of fixed size string.
Size is deduced by the first zero size string found:
    e.g. S1��� STR2� � (str_size == 5)
Each string cannot be longer than str_size - 1.
For ct.c_char and arrays of it.

functions.name: caen_libs._string.from_n_char_array
documentation:
Split a buffer of fixed size string.
Size is passed as parameter:
    e.g. S1��� STR2� (str_size == 5, n_str == 2)
Each string cannot be longer than str_size - 1.
For ct.c_char and arrays of it.

functions.name: caen_libs._string.from_n_char_array_p
documentation:
Same of from_n_char_array.
For pointers to ct.c_char, to avoid dereferences in case of zero size.

documentation: Binding of Link Types for InitSystem

classes.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Error

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Error.Code._missing_
documentation:
Sometimes library returns values not contained in the
enumerator, due to errors in the library itself. We
catch them and return UNKNOWN.
Raised when a wrapped C API function returns negative values.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper._Lib.__api_handle_errcheck
documentation:
Binding of CAENHV_GetError()

Even if the first argument is an handle, this binding
is defined as errcheck rather then a method of Device class
because actually it has to be called after a failed call.
The handle is obtained assuming it is the first argument
of the failed function.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper._Lib.auto_ptr
documentation:
Context manager to auto free on scope exit.

The returned pointer is initialized to NULL to avoid error
when freeing, in case callee function does not set the pointer.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper._Lib.evt_data_auto_ptr
documentation:
Context manager to auto free event data on scope exit

The returned pointer is initialized to NULL to avoid error
when freeing, in case callee function does not set the pointer.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.connect
documentation:
Binding of CAENHV_InitSystem()

New instances should be created with open(). This is meant to
reconnect a device closed with close().

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.close
documentation:
Binding of CAENHV_DeinitSystem()

This will also clear class cache.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.get_crate_map
documentation:
Binding of CAENHV_GetCrateMap()

This will also clear class cache, since the function
invalidates some internal stuctures of the C library.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.set_bd_param
documentation:
Binding of CAENHV_SetBdParam()

The CAEN HV Wrapper is not consistent, since it allows to pass
an array of values as input, to be set respectively to the slots
in the slot_list, but this is done only on some system types
(notably, on SY4527 and R6060 it uses only the first value of
the array for all channels in the slot_list). To make their
behavior homogeneous, we do the same also on systems supporting
different values, setting the same value on all slots. The trick
is not done on parameters of STRING type because the systems
that accept an array as input do not have any writable parameter
of that type. The trick is not done on CMD type because the
input value is ignored.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.set_ch_param
documentation:
Binding of CAENHV_SetChParam()

See comment on set_bd_param() for additional information.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.get_event_data
documentation:
Binding of CAENHV_GetEventData()

Even if the first call of the C API is a socket rather
than an handle, we do here a trick to make this anomaly
transparent to the user.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__get_prop
documentation:
Get single parameter property.

If args are defined, they are used to construct the value passed
to the C library, and could be used to catch errors (see comment
in __get_param_type). If default is set in kwargs, it returns
var_type(default) in case of PARAMPROPNOTFOUND error.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__get_param_prop
documentation:
Get all parameter properties.
Cannot be cached since minval/maxval may depend on the value of
other parameters.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__get_param_type
documentation:
Simplified version of __get_param_prop used internally to
retrieve just param type.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__get_param_mode
documentation:
Simplified version of __get_param_prop used internally to
retrieve just param mode.

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__check_events_support
documentation:
SY1527/SY2527 have a legacy version of events not supported by
this binding

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__library_event_thread
documentation: Devices with polling thread within library

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__resol_param_prop
documentation: Devices with weird Resol parameter property on numeric data

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__new_events_format
documentation:
Devices with new events format, with socket opened within the
library

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__char_p_p_str_bd_param_arg
documentation:
Devices that requires a char** as argument of get_bd_param of
type STRING

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__char_p_p_str_ch_param_arg

methods.name: caen_libs.caenhvwrapper.Device.__extended_get_param_type
documentation:
Same of __get_param_type, with a workaround for Name: even if
not being a real channel parameter, i.e. get_ch_param_prop does
not work, changes are sent as events of type PARAMETER.
